ops.text:
LANE B RESULTS (task aade0a1c — strip the Claude Code baseline from judge calls) — ALL GATES PASS; patch ready to ship from /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-wb (uncommitted; orchestrator ships).

PATCH (2 files): cc_steer/claude.py cached_judge ClaudeConfig gains tools="" + disable_slash_commands=True (docstring notes the stripped harness); tests/test_claude.py adds test_cached_judge_builds_spec_with_stripped_harness pinning the spec (mirrors the run_claude spec test). No version bumps — prompt text untouched; identity (dedup_key, role, prompt_version) verified unaffected.

PROTOCOL DEVIATION (evidence-forced): the registered max_turns=1 — and the fallback max_turns=2 — intermittently kill valid runs with subtype=error_max_turns: on the --json-schema path the structured output is emitted as a tool_use round and num_turns varies 2-4 per response (observed 4 on a clean success). max_turns=1: 13/14 rows died; max_turns=2: still died on the warmup row that max_turns=2 had passed minutes earlier (stochastic). Final NEW config carries NO max_turns — with tools="" there is no agentic surface to bound, and run_claude (plain text, no schema) correctly keeps its max_turns=1.

MEASUREMENT (bstrip_measure_v3, pre-registered protocol; sonnet/medium, n=14 summary-fidelity rows ids 1-14 from a fresh cp -c clone of _base-20260719, conc 6, warm_old+warm_new then old_a/new_c/old_b):
- COST: OLD $0.06539/row (old_a) vs NEW $0.04174/row = 0.638x, 36.2% cheaper. old_b warm replicate $0.04025.
- TOKENS (input-side = input+cache_read+cache_creation): OLD 50,376/row vs NEW 18,103/row — the ~32K/row Claude Code tool baseline is gone (output tokens unchanged: 955 vs 937).
- EQUIVALENCE (matched n=12): category old-vs-new 11/12=0.917 >= 0.750 floor (in-run old-vs-old 12/14=0.857); accept old-vs-new 12/12=1.000 (exact parity) >= old-vs-old 13/14=0.929. PASS.
- GATES: usd PASS, tok PASS, category PASS, accept PASS.

CAVEATS (report-honest):
1. Script defect fixed post-hoc: the run script's gate_accept compared raw counts across unequal denominators (12 matched vs 14) and printed False; the registered criterion is the agreement RATE — recomputed in bstrip_final.json. Arithmetic fix, not a threshold move.
2. Cross-run cache contamination deflates all absolute $/row (three runs over the same rows within the 1h ephemeral TTL; old_b even rides full row-context cache reads production never sees, which is why old_b $0.0403 < new_c $0.0417 — production judges each row once). The token delta (2.8x) is cache-independent and is the durable number; sonnet pricing on the measured envelopes puts the production steady-state win at roughly 30-40%/row, larger in relative terms on opus (large tier) where cache_read is $1.50/MTok.
3. NEW-arm transient failures: 2/14 in-run 'claude exited 1' (rows 6, 13); both succeed on standalone rerun (num_turns 4 and 3) — transient, and triage's run_verdicts already leaves failed rows unjudged for next-pass retry. OLD showed 0/28. Monitor the first nightly v7 pass for elevated JudgeError rate.
4. Spend: ~$5.5 of the $10 cap (run1 with the broken max_turns=1 NEW arm ~$2.15 incl. ~$0.62 of billed failures; killed v2 ~$0.6-1.1; diagnostics ~$0.18; final v3 ~$2.29). Script-reported figures under-count because errored calls still bill (~$0.048 each, per captured envelope) but return no cost to the harness.

Artifacts: ~/.cc-steer/experiments/BSTRIP/{scripts/bstrip_measure.py, scripts/diag.py, results/bstrip_measure.json (run1, max_turns=1 failure), results/bstrip_measure_v2.json (killed, max_turns=2 warm failure), results/bstrip_measure_v3.json (final), results/bstrip_final.json (corrected gates), results/diag/*.raw.json}. Gates on the workspace: full pytest exit 0 (x2 on final state), Linux docker ty "All checks passed!", docs n/a (cc_steer.claude not in the curated reference).
